QF.id: VI Debt Instruments and Government Securities_45

question.text: For Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs), what flexibility exists for Inter-Depository Transfers compared to other government securities?

answer.text: IDT of SGBs is permitted for any market type and settlement details
IDT of SGBs is not permitted at all
IDT of SGBs requires special RBI permission
IDT of SGBs follows the same restrictions as other G-Secs

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In case of SGB, IDT will be permitted for any market type and settlement details, unlike other G-Secs which are restricted to specific market types. **The Trap:** One might assume all G-Secs have the same IDT rules, but SGBs have broader flexibility. **Cross-Exam Context:** Sovereign Gold Bond operations are tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SGBs are more flexible for IDT purposes — they can be transferred across depositories under any market type, unlike other G-Secs.
